levels.title: the spark
levels.blurb: built an escape room in java and python as a high school intern and thought — wait, i can make computers do anything. stayed curious and never stopped pulling threads.

levels.title: robots and ribbons
levels.blurb: led an award-winning robotics team all the way to FTC Worlds. learned that the best engineering happens when you obsess over the details everyone else skips.

levels.title: going all in
levels.blurb: enrolled in CS at Fordham and immediately stacked a second degree in Data Science on top. if you're going to learn how systems think, you might as well learn how to make them predict things too.

levels.title: keeping the web honest
levels.blurb: joined Fordham's marketing team as a QA intern — testing, fixing, and improving the university website with javascript, html, and css. quiet work that taught me how much polish actually matters.

levels.title: data, for real
levels.blurb: spent a summer in dortmund, germany with DAAD building a forecasting model from scratch — time series clustering, forecast selection, accuracy KPIs. the kind of project where python stops being a language and starts being a tool you actually trust.

levels.title: the global stage
levels.blurb: represented nations at Harvard, Boston University, UCLA, NYU, and UC Berkeley in Model UN. drafted policy on cybersecurity, AI governance, and digital rights. turns out the skills that make you a good engineer — precision, persuasion, thinking two moves ahead — work just as well in a committee room.

levels.title: recognized
levels.blurb: Dean's List two years running, inducted into Alpha Sigma Nu — Fordham's Jesuit honor society for the top 1% of students. a City Council citation, a Harvard MUN certificate, and a Worlds robotics finalist title somewhere in there too.

levels.title: next quest
levels.blurb: data science intern at Irth this summer, MS in Data Science finishing 2027. the girl who built escape rooms is still building things — just bigger ones, with better data.
